---
title: "Technical Documentation"
---

:::{.hero-section style="padding-top: 3rem; padding-bottom: 2rem;"}
:::{.lead}
Explore the architectural components, APIs, and CLI workflows of the Monist Engine. This documentation is intended for systems engineers and formal verification researchers integrating First Synth technologies.
:::
:::

::::{.feature-grid}

:::{.feature-card}
### 1. The Oracle Layer (`monist-core`)
- **AST Parsing:** Transforming first-order logic.
- **Kosaraju's SCC:** Flatting semantic cycles.
- **T-Functor Synthesis:** Absorbing structural friction.
:::

:::{.feature-card}
### 2. GPU Physics Backend (`monist-comb`)
- **WGSL Compute Shaders:** Native execution on Vulkan/Metal/DX12.
- **Lock-Free Graph Rewiring:** Managing the FreeList and parallel S,K,I reduction loops.
- **Garbage Collection:** Isolated topological sweep passes.
:::

:::{.feature-card}
### 3. Formal Verification (`monist-verify`)
- **Lean 4 Integration:** Continuous SMT-LIB v2 exports.
- **RustMC Boundaries:** Tracking concurrent graph reductions.
:::

::::

## Getting Started with the CLI

To run the Monist Engine locally, utilize the included `monist-cli`:

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/FirstSynthesis/monist.git
cd monist

# Run the CPU benching suite
cargo bench -p monist-bench-cpu

# Execute a mathematical diagnostic (e.g., Burali-Forti)
cargo run --bin burali_forti
```

*For more detailed configuration options, see the [CLI Reference Guide](#).*
